back_translation: And you shall love the Lord your God and keep His charge, and His statutes, and His ordinances, and His commandments all the days.
And you shall know today, that it is not your children who did not know and who did not see the discipline of the Lord your God, His greatness, His mighty hand, and His outstretched arm,
and His signs and His works that He did in the midst of Egypt, to Pharaoh king of Egypt, and to all his land,

and what He did to the army of Egypt, to their horses and to their chariots, when He made the waters of the Sea of Reeds flow over their faces, when they pursued after you, and the Lord destroyed them, until this day,
and what He did to you in the wilderness until you came to this place,

and what He did to Dathan and to Abiram, sons of Eliab, son of Reuben, when the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their houses and their tents and every living thing that was at their feet, in the midst of all Israel.
For your eyes have seen every great work of the Lord that He did.
And you shall keep all the commandment that I am commanding you today, so that you may be strong and may enter and possess the land that you are crossing over there to possess,
and so that you may prolong days upon the land that the Lord swore to your fathers to give to them and to their seed, a land flowing with milk and honey.
For the land that you are entering there to possess is not like the land of Egypt from which you came out, where you sowed your seed and watered with your foot like a vegetable garden.
But the land that you are crossing over there to possess is a land of mountains and valleys; from the rain of the heavens it drinks water.
A land that the Lord your God cares for it; continually the eyes of the Lord your God are on it, from the beginning of the year and until the end of the year.
And it shall be, if listening you will listen to My commandments that I am commanding you today, to love the Lord your God and to serve Him with all your heart and with all your soul,
and I will give the rain of your land in its time, early and latter; and you will gather your grain and your new wine and your oil.
And I will give grass in your field for your cattle, and you will eat and be satisfied.
Watch yourselves, lest your heart be enticed and you turn aside and serve other gods and bow down to them,

and the anger of the Lord will burn against you, and He will shut up the heavens and there will be no rain, and the land will not give its produce, and you will perish quickly from upon the good land that the Lord is giving to you.
And you shall set these My words on your heart and on your soul, and you shall bind them as a sign on your hand, and they shall be as frontlets between your eyes.
And you shall teach them to your children, to speak in them, in your sitting in your house, and in your walking on the way, and in your lying down, and in your rising up.
And you shall write them on the doorposts of your house and in your gates,
so that your days may be multiplied and the days of your children upon the land that the Lord swore to your fathers to give to them, as the days of the heavens upon the earth.
For if keeping you will keep all this commandment that I am commanding you to do it, to love the Lord your God, to walk in all His ways, and to cling to Him,
then the Lord will dispossess all these nations from before you, and you will possess nations greater and mightier than you.
Every place where the sole of your foot will tread in it, to you it shall be: from the wilderness and Lebanon, from the river, the Euphrates River, and unto the western sea, shall be your border.
No man shall stand before you; your fear and your dread the Lord your God will give upon the face of all the land where you tread in it, as He has spoken to you.
See, I am setting before you today a blessing and a curse:
the blessing, if you listen to the commandments of the Lord your God that I am commanding you today;
and the curse, if you do not listen to the commandments of the Lord your God and turn aside from the way that I am commanding you today, to walk after other gods that you have not known.
And it shall be, when the Lord your God brings you to the land that you are entering there to possess, you shall give the blessing on Mount Gerizim and the curse on Mount Ebal.
Are they not across the Jordan, behind the way of the going down of the sun, in the land of the Canaanite who dwells in the Arabah, opposite Gilgal, beside the oaks of Moreh?
For you are crossing the Jordan to enter to possess the land that the Lord your God is giving to you, and you shall possess it and dwell in it,
and you shall be careful to do all the statutes and the ordinances that I am setting before you today.
